body:
## Reproduction

Hive Mind issue link-assistant/hive-mind#1860 reproduced this with start-command 0.28.0:

```sh
$ --isolated docker --detached --session repro -- solve https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1855 --tool codex
$ --status repro
```

Observed status/log details:

- `options.image` is `ubuntu:latest`
- the detached Docker log ends with `bash: solve: command not found`
- exit code is 127

That happens because the Docker backend starts a base OS image unless a caller provides an image, and there is currently no documented way to pass tool-specific bind mounts such as GitHub, Claude, or Codex auth directories.

## Expected behavior

Callers using Docker isolation should be able to configure the container runtime surface needed by the command, at least:

- image selection
- bind mounts / volumes
- environment variables
- privileged mode when a Docker-in-Docker image is intentionally used

The selected values should also be persisted in `--status` output for debugging.

## Workaround

Hive Mind is working around this by launching an explicit `docker run` command inside a tracked screen isolation session. That preserves start-command lifecycle/log tracking while letting Hive Mind choose `konard/hive-mind-dind:latest` and mount only the selected tool's credentials.

## Suggested fix

Extend the Docker backend CLI/options with arguments such as:

- `--image <image>` (already present)
- `--volume/-v <host:container[:mode]>`
- `--mount <mount-spec>`
- `--env/-e <KEY=VALUE>`
- `--privileged`

Then thread those options into the internal `docker run` argv and include them in status metadata. This would let downstream callers use native Docker isolation instead of wrapping `docker run` themselves.
title: Docker isolation needs image and bind-mount controls
